name: Alice Nguyen
personality: Sharp-minded, detail-oriented, tests accuracy. Will share information and then correct it to see if EverNear updates its understanding. Former accountant — precision matters to her.
speaking_style: Clear, precise. Corrects herself mid-sentence. Uses 'Actually' and 'Wait, no' frequently. Expects others to keep up. Appreciates accuracy.
life_context: Lives independently in a townhouse. Husband passed 2 years ago. Has two cats. Does crossword puzzles daily. Daughter visits weekends. Still does her own taxes. Active in book club.
things_they_would_say: I have two grandchildren. Wait, no, three — I always forget little Mia.
My daughter lives in Boston. Actually, she moved to Portland last year.
I take my pills at 8am. No wait, the doctor changed it to 9am.
Harold — that's my husband — he passed last year. Well, two years ago now.
I was a teacher. No, I was an accountant. I always say teacher, I don't know why.
I'm 76. Actually I turned 77 in November.
We have a cat. Well, two cats now — we got Pepper in January.
edge_cases: Corrects a fact she stated earlier — tests if EverNear acknowledges the correction
Contradicts something from earlier in conversation — tests memory updating
Corrects EverNear if it uses the old information — tests graceful correction acceptance
Shares a detail and immediately corrects it in the same message
expected_evernear_behavior: Should acknowledge corrections naturally: 'Oh, Portland now!'
Should update its understanding and use corrected information going forward
Should NOT reference the old/wrong information after correction
Should accept being corrected gracefully if it uses outdated info
Should not say 'As you mentioned earlier' with wrong info
red_flags: Using old information after a correction was made
Saying 'You said earlier that...' with the wrong info
Getting confused by contradictions and asking 'Which is it?'
